SEARCH
Package edu.bu.pas.uno

Class Game

java.lang.Object
edu.bu.pas.uno.Game
public class Game extends Object
A class that represents the state of the game. Something to note here is that agents are given their own ids which do not correspond to what order they go in the game. The turn order is a circular list where players are referenced by their "logical" ids (e.g. player 18812 may be player 2 in a 2-player game of Uno). This object (and most of the other objects related to game logic) typically refer to players by their "logical idx" rather than their "player idx" but we store a mapping from player idx -> logical idx if you wish to convert. Just be aware of what kind of index is being expected by the different methods.
See Also:
  • Game.GameView
  • Game.PlayerOrder
  • Game.UnresolvedCardBuffer
  • Game.TimeoutTracker

    • drawCard

      public final int drawCard(Hand hand)
      A method to draw a single card into the provided hand. If the draw pile is empty then the discard pile will be shuffled into it (and cleared).
      Parameters:
      hand - The Hand to draw a card into
      Returns:
      the index of the drawn card in the modified Hand

    • drawTotal

      public final void drawTotal(Hand hand, int total)
      A method to draw a specified number of cards into the provided hand. If the draw pile is empty then the discard pile will be shuffled into it (and cleared).
      Parameters:
      hand - The Hand to draw cards into.
      total - The number of cards to draw.

    • getMove

      public final Move getMove()
      A method to query the Agent who is currently playing. This method will mark timeouts depending on whether the queried Agent returns a null Move. This method is not meant for you to call on Games created from Game.GameViews as the Agent instances are not copied over.
      Returns:
      The Move returned by the queried Agent. May be null.

    • resolveMove

      public final void resolveMove(Move move)
      A method to resolve the provided Move. If the Move is not null then the effects of that Move will be applied to mutate this Game object inplace. Finally (regardless of whether the Move is null or not) the player order will be advanced and the number of moves in the game incremented.

    • isOver

      public final boolean isOver()
